ops.title: capt-hook LLM-judge prompt fixes are proven by live dispatch from the checkout, never by inline tests (which always stub the verdict)
ops.body: capt-hook inline tests can NEVER exercise an LLM judge prompt — every test event gets a stubbed verdict (`StubbedContext.wrapping`, default fire=True, `llm={...}` seeds overrides), and there is no prompt-eval CLI. A judge-prompt fix is validated by live dispatch: from the captain-hook checkout (its own packs.toml enables the general pack), `uv run capt-hook run PreToolUse` with a crafted stdin payload (`{"session_id":"<fresh uuid>","tool_name":...,"tool_input":...}`; fresh session_id per dispatch — fire state persists per session). Run a matrix: every carve-out shape must go silent, every true-positive shape must still fire, and shapes near-verbatim in the prompt's examples don't count as proof — test a variant too. Use `uv run` from the checkout, NOT `uvx --isolated` (that runs the released PyPI version). Small-judge prompts need blunt, explicitly-ordered rules: a mid-paragraph carve-out loses to a later imperative ("judge reachability") — phrase exceptions as a pre-fire check ("Before you fire, scan meta for..."). Applied 2026-07-16 fixing the review-routing nudges' escalation false fires (pack 0.24.0, v9.19.0, session fe81f529 evidence); see [[model-routing-convention]].
